_comment: Cards the price engine snapshots nightly — the watchlist's source of truth, and the feed behind the on-page Prospect Signal Board. SCOPE DECISION (Jun 26 2026): for baseball prospects we track ONE canonical card per player = the 1st Bowman Chrome AUTO. Fallback: if a prospect had NO auto in their first Bowman, track the 1st Bowman Chrome BASE instead and set cardType:"chrome-base". NOT in scope: Sapphire, parallels, or later Bowman sets — Chrome / Chrome Auto only. One card per player keeps data clean and fits the ~50-card nightly cron cap.
_setup: IDs below are REPLACE_ME placeholders — the cron skips any card whose id is still 'REPLACE_ME', so the board launches empty (graceful) until you fill them. To resolve a real SportsCardsPro product id for each player: SPORTSCARDSPRO_TOKEN=xxxx node tools/price-engine/resolve.mjs sportscardspro "<player> 2026 bowman chrome auto" — pick the row that is the 1st Bowman Chrome PROSPECT AUTO (not a parallel/Sapphire), paste its id, commit + push to main. Expand past the top-10 (add #11+ deep prospects) the same way, staying under ~50 cards.

_schema.id: string — product id on the source site
_schema.label: string — human name for display, e.g. 'Ethan Holliday — 1st Bowman Chrome Auto'
_schema.condition: loose | graded | psa10 | new | cib  (which price column to track; loose=raw)
_schema.cardType: chrome-auto (default) | chrome-base (fallback when no auto was issued)
_schema.slug: optional — site page this card links back to
_schema.rank: optional integer — editorial rank if it's a featured top-10 card (else omit)
